title: System tests: honor $OCI_RUNTIME (for CI)
body:
Some CI systems set $OCI_RUNTIME as a way to override the
default crun. Integration (e2e) tests honor this, but system
tests were not aware of the convention; this means we haven't
been testing system tests with runc, which means RHEL gating
tests are now failing.

The proper solution would be to edit containers.conf on CI
systems. Sorry, that would involve too much CI-VM work.
Instead, this PR detects $OCI_RUNTIME and creates a dummy
containers.conf file using that runtime.

Add: various skips for tests that don't work with runc.

Refactor: add a helper function so we don't need to do
the complicated 'podman info blah blah .OCIRuntime.blah'
thing in many places.

BUG: we leave a tmp file behind on exit.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
[re-pushed with a refinement: the `pid=host` failure only happens when rootless, not rootful. I updated the `skip` correspondingly, so it still runs on root tests.]

This PR has already been much more complicated than it should've been; and it's getting worse. Ubunto 2010 (which is now old-ubuntu, apparently) is now [failing](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6012390417367040/html/sys-podman-ubuntu-2010-root-host.log.html#t--00135), and it's not a flake:
```
[+0589s] not ok 135 podman volume: exec/noexec
         # (from function `die' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 412,
         #  from function `run_podman' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 220,
         #  in test file test/system/160-volumes.bats, line 131)
         #   `run_podman ${expect_rc} run --rm --volume $myvolume:/vol:noexec,z $IMAGE /vol/myscript' failed
         # # podman rm --all --force
         # # podman ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
         # # podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
         # quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210223 4abe85964f5c
         # # podman volume rm -a
         # # podman volume create myvol15CCE5Uwiv
         # myvol15CCE5Uwiv
         # # podman volume inspect --format {{.Mountpoint}} myvol15CCE5Uwiv
         # /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/myvol15CCE5Uwiv/_data
         # # podman run --rm --volume myvol15CCE5Uwiv:/vol:noexec,z quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210223 /vol/myscript
         # standard_init_linux.go:219: exec user process caused: permission denied
         # [ rc=1 (** EXPECTED 126 **) ]
         # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
         # #| FAIL: exit code is 1; expected 126
         # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```
I'll look into it tomorrow. I will also have to deal with OCI_RUNTIME=/path (I had assumed it was just the name). [UPDATE: solved, by making `podman_runtime()` return just `basename` of the runtime)
Tests green, but I need to go over each and every system-test result to confirm that nothing was skipped inadvertently; I'll do that later this morning.

Here we are. All looks good. For some reason I had thought f33 (prior fedora) would use runc; but no, it's only prior_ubuntu that uses runc. So we don't actually get any rootless runc testing. But anyway, PR is working as desired.

|             |  runtime | root   |   rootless   |    remote(root)   |
| ---         |  ------- | ----   |   --------   |    ------------   |
| fedora-33   | crun     | [ok](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5015353488048128/html/sys-podman-fedora-33-root-host.log.html) | [ok](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5965331534446592/html/sys-podman-fedora-33-rootless-host.log.html) | [ok](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4557956650893312/html/sys-remote-fedora-33-root-host.log.html) |
| fedora-34   | crun     | [ok](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6422728371601408/html/sys-podman-fedora-34-root-host.log.html) | [ok](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4839431627603968/html/sys-podman-fedora-34-rootless-host.log.html) | [ok](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6704203348312064/html/sys-remote-fedora-34-root-host.log.html) |
| **ubuntu-2010** | **runc**     | [**ok**](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5578303441469440/html/sys-podman-ubuntu-2010-root-host.log.html) | N/A | [ok](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5120906604314624/html/sys-remote-ubuntu-2010-root-host.log.html) |
| ubuntu-2104 | crun     |  [ok](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6141253394890752/html/sys-podman-ubuntu-2104-root-host.log.html) | N/A | [ok](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5683856557735936/html/sys-remote-ubuntu-2104-root-host.log.html) |

There's only one useful result, but the others, at least I didn't break anything. I also confirmed the skips, that is, that I'm not inadvertently skipping code that should run.
